title: Discord-style voice channel with always-on connection
business_context: Voice channel: users connect and stay; push-to-talk or voice-activated. Scale: 5k servers × 20 users per channel.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for channel + voice state. SFU handles audio; socket carries control.

database_design.schema: const VoiceChannelSchema=new Schema({_id:String,serverId:String,name:String,users:[{userId:String,selfMute:Boolean,serverMute:Boolean,speaking:Boolean,joinedAt:Date}],bitrate:{type:Number,default:64000}});

race_conditions_addressed: Double join: $ne guard.
Disconnect race: $pull idempotent.
Speaking flood: ephemeral broadcast, not persisted.
scaling_considerations: 10x: speaking events at high rate — throttle on client.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: stateless join. No way to know who's in channel after restart.

title: Marketing campaign live attribution dashboard
business_context: Live ad spend vs revenue attribution per campaign. Updates every 30s. Scale: 500 campaigns.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG with hourly buckets. Continuous aggregate refresh.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE ad_spend(campaign_id BIGINT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ,spend_cents BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE attributed_revenue(campaign_id BIGINT,order_id BIGINT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ,revenue_cents BIGINT);\nCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW camp_hourly AS SELECT campaign_id, date_trunc('hour',ts) AS h, sum(spend_cents) AS spend FROM ad_spend GROUP BY 1,2;

race_conditions_addressed: Refund: attribution recomputed at next tick.
Webhook delay: lazy aggregation.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: query on every render. DB scan storm.

title: Pop quiz live game with first-correct points
business_context: Kahoot-clone: host shows question; first correct answer wins points. Scale: 5k games × 500 players.
failure_modes_to_handle: Two players same answer same instant
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for game + questions + answers.

database_design.schema: const QuizSchema=new Schema({_id:String,hostId:String,currentQ:Number,questions:[{text:String,options:[String],correct:Number,revealedAt:Date}],scores:{type:Map,of:Number}});\nconst QuizAnsSchema=new Schema({gameId:String,qIdx:Number,playerId:String,answer:Number,answeredAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Cheat: correct field omitted from broadcast.
Same instant: server timestamp orders.
Late join: only see current question (no replay of past).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: send full question. Devtools reveals correct.

title: Domain backorder with snipe queue
business_context: Domain auction at drop time: bidders queue snipes; first valid bid at exact drop wins. Scale: 50k drops/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for drops + queued bids. Redis sorted set for in-flight queue per drop.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE drops(id BIGSERIAL PK,domain TEXT,drop_at TIMESTAMPTZ,winner_id BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE queued_bids(id BIGSERIAL PK,drop_id BIGINT,bidder_id BIGINT,max_cents BIGINT,queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());
database_design.queries: On drop: ORDER BY queued_at, max_cents DESC, settle.

race_conditions_addressed: Drop time exact: server-side scheduler triggers executeDrop at drop_at.
Multiple snipes: ORDER BY queued_at picks earliest.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client clock. Skew = unfair.

title: Customer-facing 'agent is typing' indicator with multi-agent fan-in
business_context: Multi-agent collab supporting one customer; customer sees 'agent typing'. Scale: 10k chats.
constraints: Privacy: customer doesn't see agent names
architecture_reasoning: 1. Redis for ephemeral typing state per chat.

database_design.schema: // Redis: HASH typing:{chatId} → agentId → expiresAt

race_conditions_addressed: Two agents: customer sees 'someone typing' regardless of count.
Privacy: aggregated, no agent name.
Throttle: in-memory per (user,chat).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: emit agent name. Privacy leak.

title: Drone aerial photo with georeferenced annotations
business_context: Survey app: aerial map; pins with GPS coords. Scale: 5k surveys.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for annotations (geoJSON). PG for survey metadata + sharing.

database_design.schema: const GeoAnnotationSchema=new Schema({_id:String,surveyId:String,geom:{type:Object},properties:Schema.Types.Mixed,authorId:String,layer:String,createdAt:Date});\nGeoAnnotationSchema.index({surveyId:1,layer:1});\nGeoAnnotationSchema.index({geom:'2dsphere'});

race_conditions_addressed: Offline edit: client queue + replay; UUID prevents dupes.
Layer perm: server checks layer access.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: pixel coords. Map zoom breaks.

title: Slack-style status with auto-clear on schedule end
business_context: User custom status with expiry. Scale: 1M users.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for current + history.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE user_status(user_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,emoji TEXT,text TEXT,expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE TABLE status_history(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,emoji TEXT,text TEXT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ);

race_conditions_addressed: Expiry: server sweeper.
Concurrent set: UPSERT last write wins.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client expiry. Restart loses expiry.

title: Manufacturing line: per-station throughput with downstream WIP
business_context: Factory line monitoring: 10 stations; each reports cycle; downstream WIP buffers. Scale: 100 factories.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo time-series + PG metadata.

database_design.schema: const CycleSchema=new Schema({stationId:String,lineId:String,ts:Date,cycleSec:Number,defect:Boolean},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'stationId'}});\nCREATE TABLE wip_buffer(station_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,count INT,threshold INT,updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ);

race_conditions_addressed: Buffer overflow: GREATEST(0) clamps.
Restart: PG durable counts.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: in-memory counts. Restart resets.

title: Live podcast recording with co-host remote join
business_context: Podcast app: host + 3 co-hosts remote; each records locally then uploads; server merges. Scale: 200 simul.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for episode + tracks.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE podcast_episodes(id BIGSERIAL PK,title TEXT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ,host_id BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE episode_tracks(episode_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ,storage_key TEXT,uploaded BOOLEAN,PRIMARY KEY(episode_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Sync drift: server-issued startedAt is reference.
Upload fail: retry via storage_key idempotent.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: server records all. Bandwidth + cost explosion.

title: Construction RFI workflow with attachment review
business_context: RFI (Request For Info): contractor asks, architect answers, both with attachments. Scale: 100k projects.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for RFIs. Mongo for attachments metadata.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE rfis(id BIGSERIAL PK,project_id BIGINT,asker_id BIGINT,status TEXT,sla_at TIMESTAMPTZ,question TEXT,answer TEXT,answered_by BIGINT);\nconst AttachmentSchema=new Schema({_id:String,rfiId:String,storageKey:String,uploaderId:String,createdAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Multiple answers: WHERE status='open' guard.
Attachment scan: separate worker; status pending.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: no status machine. RFI in unknown state.

title: Batch print job: 5000 PDF generation with worker pool
business_context: Mail merge: 5000 personalized PDFs; pool workers parallel; progress + cancel. Scale: 50 simul.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for job + per-doc status.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE print_jobs(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,total INT,done INT DEFAULT 0,failed INT DEFAULT 0,status TEXT,zip_key TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE print_docs(job_id BIGINT,idx INT,status TEXT,storage_key TEXT,error TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(job_id,idx));

race_conditions_addressed: Failure isolation: per-doc; failure doesn't stop pool.
Cancel mid-zip: zip worker checks flag too.
Partial: resume from done count.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: linear loop. 1 fail = abort all.

title: Storyboard editor with frame-by-frame collab
business_context: Animation pre-prod: shared storyboard; per-frame edits; reorder. Scale: 5k storyboards × 200 frames.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for storyboards + frames.

database_design.schema: const StoryboardSchema=new Schema({_id:String,projectId:String,name:String});\nconst FrameSchema=new Schema({_id:String,storyboardId:String,order:Number,sketchKey:String,notes:String,version:{type:Number,default:1}});\nFrameSchema.index({storyboardId:1,order:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Two reorder: bulkWrite atomic per op; second overwrites.
Lock leak: TTL 5min.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: array index reorder. Concurrent inserts shift indexes.

title: Stream chat with slow-mode and follower-only
business_context: Live stream chat: slow mode (1 msg per N sec), follower-only mode. Scale: 100k concurrent viewers per stream.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for messages + mod actions. Redis for rate limit + slow mode state.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE stream_chats(id BIGSERIAL PK,stream_id BIGINT,sender_id BIGINT,body TEXT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nCREATE TABLE stream_settings(stream_id BIGINT PRIMARY KEY,slow_mode_sec INT DEFAULT 0,followers_only BOOLEAN DEFAULT false);\nCREATE TABLE timeouts(stream_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,until TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(stream_id,user_id));
database_design.queries: Slow mode check: Redis SETNX with TTL.

race_conditions_addressed: Slow mode bypass: SETNX atomic.
Timeout race: UPSERT.
Mode change: per-msg check.
scaling_considerations: 100k viewers fan-out: shard by stream hash.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client throttle. Bypassable.

title: Tycoon-style cooperative city builder
business_context: Coop city: 4 players build together; shared budget; turn-based actions. Scale: 5k cities.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for city state. PG for save snapshots.

database_design.schema: const CitySchema=new Schema({_id:String,players:[String],budget:Number,tiles:[Schema.Types.Mixed],turn:Number,currentPlayer:String,version:{type:Number,default:1}});\nCREATE TABLE city_saves(id BIGSERIAL PK,city_id TEXT,snapshot JSONB,saved_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Budget race: tx ensures atomic.
Turn race: currentPlayer check inside tx.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client computes cost. Cheaters build free.

title: Multi-item bundle auction
business_context: Bundle deal: bid on all items or pass; max bidder wins entire bundle. Scale: 1k bundles/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for bundles + items + bids. Tx for settlement.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE bundles(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,reserve_cents BIGINT,current_bid_cents BIGINT DEFAULT 0,current_bidder_id BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE bundle_items(bundle_id BIGINT,item_id BIGINT,PRIMARY KEY(bundle_id,item_id));\nCREATE TABLE items(id BIGSERIAL PK,owner_id BIGINT,status TEXT);
database_design.queries: Bid: update current. Settle: transfer all items in tx.

race_conditions_addressed: Item sold separately: FOR UPDATE on items inside bid tx.
Concurrent bids: advisory lock.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: bid without item check. Settlement fails on some items.

title: Game lobby with quick-match indication of activity
business_context: Game lobby browser: rooms show 'players currently in', 'active 30s ago'. Scale: 50k rooms.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for rooms + activity field updated on event.

database_design.schema: const GameRoomSchema=new Schema({_id:String,gameType:String,playerIds:[String],lastActivityAt:Date,activeUserCount:Number,maxPlayers:Number});\nGameRoomSchema.index({gameType:1,lastActivityAt:-1});
database_design.queries: Browse: filter by type + activity > 30s ago.

race_conditions_addressed: Stale: filter by lastActivityAt > 30s.
Cleanup: TTL on lastActivityAt indirect via filter.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: full scan every browse. DB scan storm.

title: Audio transcript review with timestamped corrections
business_context: Transcription review: editor adjusts text per timestamp. Scale: 50k transcripts.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for segments with text.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE transcripts(id BIGSERIAL PK,audio_id TEXT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE transcript_segments(id BIGSERIAL PK,transcript_id BIGINT,start_ms INT,end_ms INT,text TEXT,version INT DEFAULT 1,edited_by BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE segment_history(id BIGSERIAL PK,segment_id BIGINT,text TEXT,version INT,edited_by BIGINT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent: version check.
History per edit.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: blob text. Diff impossible.

title: Outbox pattern for cross-service event distribution
business_context: Microservices: events written to local outbox; relay to message broker; consumers process. Scale: 50k events/sec.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG outbox table + business tx. Relay worker drains to Kafka/Redis. Sockets fan out to subscribers.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE outbox(id BIGSERIAL PK,aggregate_id TEXT,event_type TEXT,payload JSONB,created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),published_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE INDEX ON outbox(published_at) WHERE published_at IS NULL;

race_conditions_addressed: Atomic: outbox insert in same tx as business write.
Dup: id field for consumer dedup.
Relay crash: published_at NULL → re-process.
scaling_considerations: 50k/sec: relay worker pool; per-aggregate ordering preserved by id.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: emit directly from service. Crash between business write + emit = lost event.

title: Bee hive monitor with weight/temp/sound
business_context: Apiary monitoring: per-hive sensors; alert on swarm prep. Scale: 10k hives.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo time-series. PG for hive metadata.

database_design.schema: const HiveSampleSchema=new Schema({hiveId:String,ts:Date,weight:Number,tempC:Number,soundDb:Number},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'hiveId'}});\nCREATE TABLE hives(id BIGSERIAL PK,beekeeper_id BIGINT,location TEXT,thresholds JSONB);

race_conditions_addressed: Cold snap: per-season thresholds.
Sensor offline: ingest gap visible.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: hardcoded threshold. Beekeeper false alerts.

title: Background blur indicator + state sync
business_context: Video call: blur on/off + virtual background selection synced as 'visual state'. Scale: 1k calls.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for participant settings; broadcast via socket.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE call_settings(call_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,blur BOOLEAN,bg_image_key TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(call_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Selection mismatch: UPSERT last write wins.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: don't sync. Peer sees no indicator of effects.

title: Tournament registration with team formation deadline
business_context: Esports tournament: teams form before deadline; captain creates, invites players. Scale: 10k tournaments.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for tournament + teams + members.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE tournaments(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,deadline TIMESTAMPTZ,locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,team_size INT);\nCREATE TABLE teams(id BIGSERIAL PK,tournament_id BIGINT,name TEXT,captain_id BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE team_members(team_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,status TEXT,PRIMARY KEY(team_id,user_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Team full: count check inside lock.
Deadline: server-time.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client check size. Race fills 11th seat.

title: Multi-region eventually-consistent order replication
business_context: E-commerce in 3 regions; order placed in any region, replicated to others. Scale: 100k orders/day per region.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG per region for orders. CRDT-like merge logic for conflicts; Mongo for audit/global view.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE orders(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,region TEXT,status TEXT,total_cents BIGINT,updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,version INT);\nCREATE TABLE replication_log(id BIGSERIAL PK,order_id TEXT,from_region TEXT,payload JSONB,ts TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nconst GlobalOrderAuditSchema=new Schema({_id:String,orderId:String,region:String,action:String,payload:Schema.Types.Mixed,ts:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Concurrent regions: LWW + region tiebreak.
Partition: replication_log durable; replay after heal.
Edit conflict: version check.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: synchronous cross-region. 200ms latency per write.

title: Recipe collaborative editing with ingredient quantity sync
business_context: Recipe site: collaborate on recipe; ingredient scaling. Scale: 50k recipes.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for recipes; ingredient subdoc.

database_design.schema: const RecipeSchema=new Schema({_id:String,title:String,servings:Number,ingredients:[{name:String,qty:Number,unit:String,version:Number}],steps:[{order:Number,text:String}],version:Number});

race_conditions_addressed: Per-ingredient version.
Scale + edit: scale bumps ingredient versions; subsequent edit must use new version.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client computes scale. Drift.

title: School parent-teacher chat with translation per message
business_context: School app: parents chat with teachers; auto-translated. Scale: 5M users.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Reuse sock-074 pattern.

database_design.schema: -- as sock-074.

backend_solution: // Equivalent to sock-074 but with quiet hours check before notification dispatch (in-app still allowed).
frontend_solution: // Equivalent to sock-074 frontend.
race_conditions_addressed: Quiet hours: notification fallback suppressed but in-app chat works.
Lang change: re-translates on next message.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: no quiet hours. Teacher gets 2am pings.

title: Capstone: virtual conference platform with stages, expo, networking
business_context: Virtual conf: 10 stages (talks), expo booths, 1:1 networking randoms. 50k attendees. Scale: 1 mega-event.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Multi-namespace. PG for event + booths. Mongo for chats. Redis for active state.

database_design.queries: Random pair: SELECT users in queue, pair.

race_conditions_addressed: Booth full: capacity check atomic in UPDATE.
Pair race: SRANDMEMBER + SREM atomic-ish; possible double-match — handled by INSERT idempotency check.
Disconnect cleanup: per-namespace handler.
scaling_considerations: 50k attendees: stage fan-out via Redis adapter. Networking queue per event scales by hashing.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: single namespace for all. Booth chat floods stage. Killer: capacity check race overflows booth.
